Title: ADEN

ADEN

Previously known as Oud Fire

Warm oud, fire-roasted amber

The port city exhales in layers — salt-crusted timber, coal smoke curling from dockside braziers, the honeyed amber of frankincense stacked in merchant stalls. Aden has traded in fire since before memory. You press your wrist to warm stone and the oud rises like a signal flame, dark and unhurried, carrying the weight of every cargo that ever passed through these ancient walls.

Title: SAANA
Body (HTML):
SAANA

Previously known as Honey Oud

Raw honey, aged oud, desert spice

The old city rises at seven thousand feet, its tower houses built of rammed earth and gypsum, their windows glowing like amber lanterns against a desert sky. In the souks of Sana'a, wild honey arrives in clay vessels from the highlands — dark, floral, thick with the memory of mountain wildflowers. The oud here is sun-warmed and golden, sweetened by altitude and time. Saana is the scent of honey dripped onto ancient wood, of warmth earned through elevation, of a city that has been keeping secrets since before the caravans arrived.

Title: MALABAR

MALABAR

The Malabar Coast unfurls like a green ribbon along the Arabian Sea — pepper vines climbing teak forests, cardamom drying on stone terraces, vanilla orchids threading through the canopy. The air here is heavy with resin and sweetness, vanilla warmed by monsoon rain, balsam carried on salt wind. Malabar is the scent of origin itself — the coast where the world first learned to trade in beauty.

Title: BEIRUT
Body (HTML):
BEIRUT

Blood mandarin, cinnamon, leather

The city rebuilds itself every generation and somehow comes back more beautiful. There is a specific glamour here — earned, not inherited. Spiced leather and amber warmed by skin that has danced until the call to prayer. Beirut is the scent of resilience dressed in silk, of a city that refuses to stop celebrating even when the walls shake.

Carmel is the scent of understatement — the whispered confidence of a woman who has never once raised her voice to be heard.

RIYADH

Previously known as Black Oud

Arabian oud, noble wood

The palace doors close and the room fills with oud — not the tourist variety, but the real thing, aged in crystal decanters for years. In Riyadh, oud is status, devotion, identity. It rises from heated bukhoor, settling into white thobes and silk abayas with a nobility that cannot be faked. This is Arabian oud at its most uncompromising: dark, noble, ancient.

Title: SAMARKAND
Body (HTML):
SAMARKAND

Previously known as Oud Aura

Royal oud, ceremonial wood

The Registan's turquoise domes shimmer in the Central Asian sun and the Silk Road ghosts are everywhere — merchants who carried oud westward, wrapping it in silk alongside saffron and lapis lazuli. Samarkand is the crossroads of civilization: ceremonial oud, warm wood, the dusty grandeur of an empire that connected East and West through trade, trust, and fragrance.

Title: SICILY
Body (HTML):
SICILY

Previously known as Sicilian Oudh

Citrus grove, Mediterranean oud

The citrus groves climb the volcanic slopes and the air is electric — bergamot, blood orange, the mineral breath of Etna's black soil. Then the oud arrives, Mediterranean and unexpected, softened by salt wind and wild fennel. Sicily is where the grove meets the sea and the oud finds its warmth, yielding a fragrance that is both ancient and impossibly alive.
